item.title: Scaling Up Occupancy-centric Driving Scene Generation: Dataset and Method
item.dc:creator: Bohan Li
Xin Jin
Hu Zhu
Hongsi Liu
Ruikai Li
Jiazhe Guo
Kaiwen Cai
Chao Ma
Yueming Jin
Hao Zhao
Xiaokang Yang
Wenjun Zeng
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Driving scene generation is a critical domain for autonomous driving, enabling downstream applications, including perception and planning evaluation. Occupancy-centric methods have recently achieved state-of-the-art results by offering consistent conditioning across frames and modalities; however, their performance heavily depends on annotated occupancy data, which still remains scarce. To overcome this limitation, we curate Nuplan-Occ, the largest semantic occupancy dataset to date, constructed from the widely used Nuplan benchmark. Its scale and diversity facilitate not only large-scale generative modeling but also autonomous driving downstream applications. Based on this dataset, we develop a unified framework that jointly synthesizes high-quality semantic occupancy, multi-view videos, and LiDAR point clouds. Our approach incorporates a spatio-temporal disentangled architecture to support high-fidelity spatial expansion and temporal forecasting of 4D dynamic occupancy. To bridge modal gaps, we further propose two novel techniques: a Gaussian splatting-based sparse point map rendering strategy that enhances multi-view video generation, and a sensor-aware embedding strategy that explicitly models LiDAR sensor properties for realistic multi-LiDAR simulation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves superior generation fidelity and scalability compared to existing approaches, and validates its practical value in downstream tasks. Published: 2026-05-22T19:36:11+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.813 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Bohan Li; Xin Jin; Hu Zhu; Hongsi Liu; Ruikai Li; Jiazhe Guo; Kaiwen Cai; Chao Ma; Yueming Jin; Hao Zhao; Xiaokang Yang; Wenjun Zeng

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3696126

Relevance: 0.813 (must_read)

Driving scene generation is a critical domain for autonomous driving, enabling downstream applications, including perception and planning evaluation. Occupancy-centric methods have recently achieved state-of-the-art results by offering consistent conditioning across frames and modalities; however, their performance heavily depends on annotated occupancy data, which still remains scarce. To overcome this limitation, we curate Nuplan-Occ, the largest semantic occupancy dataset to date, constructed from the widely used Nuplan benchmark. Its scale and diversity facilitate not only large-scale generative modeling but also autonomous driving downstream applications. Based on this dataset, we develop a unified framework that jointly synthesizes high-quality semantic occupancy, multi-view videos, and LiDAR point clouds. Our approach incorporates a spatio-temporal disentangled architecture to support high-fidelity spatial expansion and temporal forecasting of 4D dynamic occupancy. To bridge modal gaps, we further propose two novel techniques: a Gaussian splatting-based sparse point map rendering strategy that enhances multi-view video generation, and a sensor-aware embedding strategy that explicitly models LiDAR sensor properties for realistic multi-LiDAR simulation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves superior generation fidelity and scalability compared to existing approaches, and validates its practical value in downstream tasks.

item.title: Complementary Masking and Cyclic Training for Unsupervised Domain Adaptive Object Detection
item.dc:creator: Jing Teng
Zhiwei Zhu
Xujie Long
Mengyang Pu
Tian Wang
Ruifeng Shi
You Lv
Hichem Snoussi
Jonathan Li
item.description: Unsupervised Domain Adaptive Object Detection (UDAOD) transfers knowledge from a labeled source domain to an unlabeled target domain, which boosts the adaptability of detectors across diverse data distributions. While advances in the teacher-student framework show significant promise for bridging domain gaps, two challenges remain: the unreliability of the pre-trained model due to target domain ignorance, and the negative impact caused by low-quality pseudo-labels. To address these challenges, we propose a two-stage teacher-student framework, termed Complementary Masking and Cyclic Training (CMCT). Specifically, we introduce a complementary masking strategy in the pre-training stage by minimizing the representation discrepancy between the teacher and student networks for same objects. This enables target domain knowledge learning and improves the reliability of the pre-trained model. In the subsequent self-training stage, we adopt a cyclic training strategy with iterative mixed pseudo-label training and adversarial feature alignment. This strategy alleviates the negative impact of low-quality pseudo-labels while providing more reliable supervision signals. Extensive experiments on three cross-domain scenarios, including Cityscapes ightarrow ightarrow Foggy Cityscapes, Sim10K ightarrow ightarrow Cityscapes, and Cityscapes ightarrow ightarrow BDD100K, demonstrate that the proposed CMCT achieves competitive performance. In particular, the method obtains 53.4% mAP _{@50} _{@50} in the Cityscapes ightarrow ightarrow Foggy Cityscapes scenario, exceeding the previous performance of 52.5% mAP _{@50} _{@50} . Published: 2026-05-22T19:36:34+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.808 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jing Teng; Zhiwei Zhu; Xujie Long; Mengyang Pu; Tian Wang; Ruifeng Shi; You Lv; Hichem Snoussi; Jonathan Li

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3696141

Relevance: 0.808 (must_read)

Unsupervised Domain Adaptive Object Detection (UDAOD) transfers knowledge from a labeled source domain to an unlabeled target domain, which boosts the adaptability of detectors across diverse data distributions. While advances in the teacher-student framework show significant promise for bridging domain gaps, two challenges remain: the unreliability of the pre-trained model due to target domain ignorance, and the negative impact caused by low-quality pseudo-labels. To address these challenges, we propose a two-stage teacher-student framework, termed Complementary Masking and Cyclic Training (CMCT). Specifically, we introduce a complementary masking strategy in the pre-training stage by minimizing the representation discrepancy between the teacher and student networks for same objects. This enables target domain knowledge learning and improves the reliability of the pre-trained model. In the subsequent self-training stage, we adopt a cyclic training strategy with iterative mixed pseudo-label training and adversarial feature alignment. This strategy alleviates the negative impact of low-quality pseudo-labels while providing more reliable supervision signals. Extensive experiments on three cross-domain scenarios, including Cityscapes ightarrow ightarrow Foggy Cityscapes, Sim10K ightarrow ightarrow Cityscapes, and Cityscapes ightarrow ightarrow BDD100K, demonstrate that the proposed CMCT achieves competitive performance. In particular, the method obtains 53.4% mAP _{@50} _{@50} in the Cityscapes ightarrow ightarrow Foggy Cityscapes scenario, exceeding the previous performance of 52.5% mAP _{@50} _{@50} .

item.title: RUMPL: Ray-Based Transformers for Universal Multi-View 2D to 3D Human Pose Lifting
item.dc:creator: Seyed Abolfazl Ghasemzadeh
Alexandre Alahi
Christophe De Vleeschouwer
item.description: Estimating 3D human poses from 2D images remains challenging due to occlusions and projective ambiguity. Multi-view learning-based approaches mitigate these issues but often fail to generalize to real-world scenarios, as large-scale multi-view datasets with 3D ground truth are scarce and captured under constrained conditions. To overcome this limitation, recent methods rely on 2D pose estimation combined with 2D-to-3D pose lifting trained on synthetic data. Building on our previous MPL framework, we propose RUMPL, a transformer-based 3D pose lifter that introduces a 3D ray-based representation of 2D keypoints. This formulation enables a model agnostic to camera parameters that can be universally deployed across arbitrary camera configurations in a given area without retraining or fine-tuning. A new View Fusion Transformer leverages learned fused-ray tokens to aggregate information along rays, further improving multi-view consistency. Evaluation on standard benchmarks shows that RUMPL significantly outperforms existing methods, yielding a 56.6% MPJPE (All KP) reduction on Human3.6M over triangulation-based methods and exceeding 70% improvement on the CMU Panoptic dataset when compared to transformer-based image-representation approaches. Results on new benchmarks, including in-the-wild multi-view and multi-person datasets, confirm its robustness and scalability. Published: 2026-05-22T19:37:47+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.807 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Seyed Abolfazl Ghasemzadeh; Alexandre Alahi; Christophe De Vleeschouwer

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3694127

Relevance: 0.807 (must_read)

Estimating 3D human poses from 2D images remains challenging due to occlusions and projective ambiguity. Multi-view learning-based approaches mitigate these issues but often fail to generalize to real-world scenarios, as large-scale multi-view datasets with 3D ground truth are scarce and captured under constrained conditions. To overcome this limitation, recent methods rely on 2D pose estimation combined with 2D-to-3D pose lifting trained on synthetic data. Building on our previous MPL framework, we propose RUMPL, a transformer-based 3D pose lifter that introduces a 3D ray-based representation of 2D keypoints. This formulation enables a model agnostic to camera parameters that can be universally deployed across arbitrary camera configurations in a given area without retraining or fine-tuning. A new View Fusion Transformer leverages learned fused-ray tokens to aggregate information along rays, further improving multi-view consistency. Evaluation on standard benchmarks shows that RUMPL significantly outperforms existing methods, yielding a 56.6% MPJPE (All KP) reduction on Human3.6M over triangulation-based methods and exceeding 70% improvement on the CMU Panoptic dataset when compared to transformer-based image-representation approaches. Results on new benchmarks, including in-the-wild multi-view and multi-person datasets, confirm its robustness and scalability.

item.title: A Guided Fusion Network based on Cross-Scale Semantic Alignment for multi-spectral object detection
item.dc:creator: Hongtao Wu
Jing Rong
Qiuzhan Zhou
Di Yang
Weiqi Wang
item.description: Multi-spectral object detection gains significant attention due to its potential in all-weather applications, particularly those involving RGB and Infrared (IR) images. However, existing methods fail to account for the semantic differences between the RGB and IR modalities, resulting in insufficient modal feature fusion and limiting the performance of RGB-IR object detection. To address these issues, we propose a novel learning network, the Semantic Alignment Guided Fusion Network (SAGF-Net), that fuses RGB and IR images in a guided manner to extract more complementary feature representations. Our network consists of three key modules: Cross-Scale Semantic Alignment (CSSA), Cross-Modal Information Guidance (CMIG), and Deformable Cross-Attention (DCA). The CSSA module alleviates semantic differences across different receptive fields through cross-scale dynamic attention fusion. Subsequently, the CMIG module extracts modality-specific advantageous features and guides the other modality to enhance the expression of key information, obtaining refined and enhanced feature representations. To reduce computational complexity, we introduce the DCA module, which selects only partial keys and values for each query within a local region of the feature map. Extensive comparative experiments and ablation experiments on multiple public datasets achieve state-of-the-art performance, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed method. Published: 2026-05-23T15:07:22+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.807 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Hongtao Wu; Jing Rong; Qiuzhan Zhou; Di Yang; Weiqi Wang

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.114024

Relevance: 0.807 (must_read)

Multi-spectral object detection gains significant attention due to its potential in all-weather applications, particularly those involving RGB and Infrared (IR) images. However, existing methods fail to account for the semantic differences between the RGB and IR modalities, resulting in insufficient modal feature fusion and limiting the performance of RGB-IR object detection. To address these issues, we propose a novel learning network, the Semantic Alignment Guided Fusion Network (SAGF-Net), that fuses RGB and IR images in a guided manner to extract more complementary feature representations. Our network consists of three key modules: Cross-Scale Semantic Alignment (CSSA), Cross-Modal Information Guidance (CMIG), and Deformable Cross-Attention (DCA). The CSSA module alleviates semantic differences across different receptive fields through cross-scale dynamic attention fusion. Subsequently, the CMIG module extracts modality-specific advantageous features and guides the other modality to enhance the expression of key information, obtaining refined and enhanced feature representations. To reduce computational complexity, we introduce the DCA module, which selects only partial keys and values for each query within a local region of the feature map. Extensive comparative experiments and ablation experiments on multiple public datasets achieve state-of-the-art performance, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed method.

item.title: FocalClick-XL: Towards Unified and High-quality Interactive Segmentation
item.dc:creator: Xi Chen
Hengshuang Zhao
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Interactive segmentation enables users to extract binary masks of target objects through simple interactions such as clicks, scribbles, and boxes. However, existing methods often support only limited interaction forms and struggle to capture fine details. In this paper, we revisit the classical coarse-to-fine design of FocalClick and introduce significant extensions. Inspired by its multi-stage strategy, we propose a novel pipeline, FocalClick-XL , to address these challenges simultaneously. Following the emerging trend of large-scale pretraining, we decompose interactive segmentation into meta-tasks that capture different levels of information (context, object, and detail), and assign a dedicated subnet to each level. This decomposition allows each subnet to undergo scaled pretraining with independent data and supervision, maximizing its effectiveness. To enhance flexibility, we share context- and detail-level information across different interaction forms as common knowledge while introducing a prompting layer at the object level to encode specific interaction types. As a result, FocalClick-XL achieves state-of-the-art performance on click-based benchmarks and demonstrates remarkable adaptability to diverse interaction formats, including boxes, scribbles, and coarse masks. Beyond binary mask generation, it is also capable of predicting alpha mattes with fine-grained details, making it a versatile and powerful tool for interactive segmentation. Published: 2026-05-22T19:36:11+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.804 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xi Chen; Hengshuang Zhao

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3695885

Relevance: 0.804 (must_read)

Interactive segmentation enables users to extract binary masks of target objects through simple interactions such as clicks, scribbles, and boxes. However, existing methods often support only limited interaction forms and struggle to capture fine details. In this paper, we revisit the classical coarse-to-fine design of FocalClick and introduce significant extensions. Inspired by its multi-stage strategy, we propose a novel pipeline, FocalClick-XL , to address these challenges simultaneously. Following the emerging trend of large-scale pretraining, we decompose interactive segmentation into meta-tasks that capture different levels of information (context, object, and detail), and assign a dedicated subnet to each level. This decomposition allows each subnet to undergo scaled pretraining with independent data and supervision, maximizing its effectiveness. To enhance flexibility, we share context- and detail-level information across different interaction forms as common knowledge while introducing a prompting layer at the object level to encode specific interaction types. As a result, FocalClick-XL achieves state-of-the-art performance on click-based benchmarks and demonstrates remarkable adaptability to diverse interaction formats, including boxes, scribbles, and coarse masks. Beyond binary mask generation, it is also capable of predicting alpha mattes with fine-grained details, making it a versatile and powerful tool for interactive segmentation.

item.title: PolSAR vehicle recognition via scattering mechanism-driven hybrid attention
item.dc:creator: Jie Deng
Wei Wang
Huiqiang Zhang
Deliang Xiang
Jun Zhang
item.description: Polarimetric synthetic aperture radar (PolSAR), as an advanced remote sensing technology, can provide rich polarization information and is of great significance for target recognition in complex environments. However, deep learning methods based on neural networks often fall into the dilemma of convergence or over-fitting due to insufficient samples and weak physical perception. To address this limitation, we propose a PolSAR vehicle target recognition method via scattering mechanism-driven hybrid attention. Firstly, we introduce a physics-inspired neural module, termed the Polarization Knowledge Extraction Module (PKEM), which retrieves physical scattering information from the Discrete Null-Pol Synthesis Pattern (DNSP). We then propose a Polarization Guided Feature Enhancement (PGFE) module, which dynamically re-weights deep features according to the physical prior information, thereby enhancing the model’s physical perception capabilities. Finally, we constructed a PolSAR vehicle target recognition dataset that can significantly facilitate the advancement of PolSAR ATR. Extensive experimental results on our self-collected measured dataset and Gotcha dataset confirm the superior effectiveness of the proposed physics-inspired target recognition framework. Our data will be available at https://github.com/DJJJJJJ/SMG_PolSAR . Published: 2026-05-23T15:40:00+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.803 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jie Deng; Wei Wang; Huiqiang Zhang; Deliang Xiang; Jun Zhang

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.114061

Relevance: 0.803 (must_read)

Polarimetric synthetic aperture radar (PolSAR), as an advanced remote sensing technology, can provide rich polarization information and is of great significance for target recognition in complex environments. However, deep learning methods based on neural networks often fall into the dilemma of convergence or over-fitting due to insufficient samples and weak physical perception. To address this limitation, we propose a PolSAR vehicle target recognition method via scattering mechanism-driven hybrid attention. Firstly, we introduce a physics-inspired neural module, termed the Polarization Knowledge Extraction Module (PKEM), which retrieves physical scattering information from the Discrete Null-Pol Synthesis Pattern (DNSP). We then propose a Polarization Guided Feature Enhancement (PGFE) module, which dynamically re-weights deep features according to the physical prior information, thereby enhancing the model’s physical perception capabilities. Finally, we constructed a PolSAR vehicle target recognition dataset that can significantly facilitate the advancement of PolSAR ATR. Extensive experimental results on our self-collected measured dataset and Gotcha dataset confirm the superior effectiveness of the proposed physics-inspired target recognition framework. Our data will be available at https://github.com/DJJJJJJ/SMG_PolSAR .

item.title: Point-RMAE: Reinforcement Masked Autoencoder for 3D Representation Learning
item.dc:creator: Haozhe Cheng
Lintong Wei
Wenjing Wang
Wenbiao Yan
Jinqian Chen
Jian Lu
Kun Yue
Jihua Zhu
item.description: The Mainstream 3D masked point modeling representation learning community typically employs predefined, fixed-ratio random or block masking strategies, aiming to obtain optimal representations and achieve high downstream performance. However, these empirical designs overlook the significant geometric information and structural importance differences that are inherent among different 3D points, leading to a suboptimal trade-off between the representation capture capabilities and reconstruction difficulty of such masking strategies. To address this issue, we are the first to present this decision-making problem to a reinforcement learning agent and propose a Reinforcement Masked Autoencoder for 3D representation learning, named Point-RMAE. Guided by geometric features as state factor, this method leverages the Masking Strategy Analyzer and the Dynamic Masking Generator to adaptively decide and apply the masking strategy during pretraining. The Masking Ratio Scheduling module dynamically adjusts the masking ratio based on the optimal strategy. Subsequently, the analyzer is updated by multiscale rewards derived from reconstruction quality level, distribution-aware feedback, and policy exploration. Notably, to enrich the Reward Function with distribution-aware signals and avoid decision collapse issue, we propose a Flow Matching Point Cloud Fast Generator that guides the selected masking decisions. Our method achieves outstanding performance across downstream tasks such as shape classification, medical diagnosis, object detection, action recognition, denoising and multiscale scene segmentation on ten popular 3D and 4D datasets. More importantly, Point-RMAE pioneers the application of reinforcement learning in 3D self-supervised representation learning. Published: 2026-05-22T19:37:47+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.794 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Haozhe Cheng; Lintong Wei; Wenjing Wang; Wenbiao Yan; Jinqian Chen; Jian Lu; Kun Yue; Jihua Zhu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3694193

Relevance: 0.794 (must_read)

The Mainstream 3D masked point modeling representation learning community typically employs predefined, fixed-ratio random or block masking strategies, aiming to obtain optimal representations and achieve high downstream performance. However, these empirical designs overlook the significant geometric information and structural importance differences that are inherent among different 3D points, leading to a suboptimal trade-off between the representation capture capabilities and reconstruction difficulty of such masking strategies. To address this issue, we are the first to present this decision-making problem to a reinforcement learning agent and propose a Reinforcement Masked Autoencoder for 3D representation learning, named Point-RMAE. Guided by geometric features as state factor, this method leverages the Masking Strategy Analyzer and the Dynamic Masking Generator to adaptively decide and apply the masking strategy during pretraining. The Masking Ratio Scheduling module dynamically adjusts the masking ratio based on the optimal strategy. Subsequently, the analyzer is updated by multiscale rewards derived from reconstruction quality level, distribution-aware feedback, and policy exploration. Notably, to enrich the Reward Function with distribution-aware signals and avoid decision collapse issue, we propose a Flow Matching Point Cloud Fast Generator that guides the selected masking decisions. Our method achieves outstanding performance across downstream tasks such as shape classification, medical diagnosis, object detection, action recognition, denoising and multiscale scene segmentation on ten popular 3D and 4D datasets. More importantly, Point-RMAE pioneers the application of reinforcement learning in 3D self-supervised representation learning.

item.title: Velocity First? Rethinking 3D Object Detection with 4D Millimeter Wave Radar
item.dc:creator: Changxian Zeng
Wenbo Chu
Lili Fan
Yulong Ding
Yue Tian
Xiaolin Tang
Keqiang Li
item.description: Propelled by advances in autonomous driving, accurate vehicle perception is essential for reliable decision-making. Compared with LiDAR, 4D millimeter-wave radar (4D MMW) provides stronger robustness under adverse conditions, lower deployment cost, and direct velocity measurements, motivating increasing research. However, its sparse point clouds limit scene description. This work presents Velo3DF (3D Velocity-aware Multimodal Fusion Network), a radar-camera fusion model that incorporates 3D velocity vectors into 4D MMW-based 3D detection. To mitigate positional redundancy and better utilize velocity information, a lightweight Velocity-Component-Aware module is introduced, compatible with backbones such as PointPillars. For cross-modal integration, an Alignment-Fusion module performs geometric alignment and deep fusion of image and point-cloud features. A single-modality variant, Velo3DF-R, contains 0.702M parameters and runs at 74.02 FPS while maintaining competitive accuracy. Our code is open-sourced at: https://github.com/luoyoucode/Velo3DFusion. Published: 2026-05-22T19:36:34+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.794 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Changxian Zeng; Wenbo Chu; Lili Fan; Yulong Ding; Yue Tian; Xiaolin Tang; Keqiang Li

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3696138

Relevance: 0.794 (must_read)

Propelled by advances in autonomous driving, accurate vehicle perception is essential for reliable decision-making. Compared with LiDAR, 4D millimeter-wave radar (4D MMW) provides stronger robustness under adverse conditions, lower deployment cost, and direct velocity measurements, motivating increasing research. However, its sparse point clouds limit scene description. This work presents Velo3DF (3D Velocity-aware Multimodal Fusion Network), a radar-camera fusion model that incorporates 3D velocity vectors into 4D MMW-based 3D detection. To mitigate positional redundancy and better utilize velocity information, a lightweight Velocity-Component-Aware module is introduced, compatible with backbones such as PointPillars. For cross-modal integration, an Alignment-Fusion module performs geometric alignment and deep fusion of image and point-cloud features. A single-modality variant, Velo3DF-R, contains 0.702M parameters and runs at 74.02 FPS while maintaining competitive accuracy. Our code is open-sourced at: https://github.com/luoyoucode/Velo3DFusion.

item.title: XFuse: Multi-sensor CNN transformer fusion with cross attention and quality aware curriculum learning for high resolution fractional snow cover mapping
item.dc:creator: Getnet Demil
Mourad Oussalah
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: Accurate fractional snow cover (FSC) mapping requires combining the complementary strengths of optical imagery (spectral discrimination) and Synthetic Aperture Radar (all-weather capability). Yet existing fusion approaches employ simplistic concatenation strategies that fail to capture complex inter-modal relationships, while taking into account the resolution mismatch between high-resolution inputs and coarse reference labels. This paper introduces XFuse , a hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture that addresses these challenges through three key contributions: (1) bidirectional cross-attention fusion that explicitly models SAR-optical interdependencies via query-key–value transformations, enabling each modality to selectively attend to complementary information from the other; (2) a multi-resolution training strategy ( 10 m → 60 m → 10 m " role="presentation"> 10 m → 60 m → 10 m 10 m → 60 m → 10 m ) that fuses Sentinel-1/2 features at native resolution, aggregates to 60 m for supervision against Gap-Filled Fractional Snow Cover (GFSC) labels, and disaggregates through guided upsampling to restore fine spatial structure at 10 m " role="presentation"> 10 m 10 m ; and (3) quality-aware curriculum learning that incorporates multi-source metadata (cloud masks, atmospheric parameters, quality control flags) through adaptive loss weighting, enabling robust training from heterogeneous satellite data while providing calibrated uncertainty estimates. Evaluation on 29 Sentinel-1/2 tiles from Northern Finland (18,117 test patches from 4 geographically unseen tiles) demonstrates state-of-the-art performance: 13.77% MAE and 94.66% classification accuracy, representing 38.7% MAE reduction over the best baseline. Ablation studies confirm cross-attention as the largest individual contributor (5.16 percentage point accuracy gain), while uncertainty quantification achieves excellent calibration (ECE=0.025). The methodology generalizes to earth observation applications requiring fusion of heterogeneous multi-resolution satellite data Published: 2026-05-23T00:33:24+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.792 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Getnet Demil; Mourad Oussalah

Journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1016/j.isprsjprs.2026.05.028

Relevance: 0.792 (must_read)

Accurate fractional snow cover (FSC) mapping requires combining the complementary strengths of optical imagery (spectral discrimination) and Synthetic Aperture Radar (all-weather capability). Yet existing fusion approaches employ simplistic concatenation strategies that fail to capture complex inter-modal relationships, while taking into account the resolution mismatch between high-resolution inputs and coarse reference labels. This paper introduces XFuse , a hybrid CNN-Transformer architecture that addresses these challenges through three key contributions: (1) bidirectional cross-attention fusion that explicitly models SAR-optical interdependencies via query-key–value transformations, enabling each modality to selectively attend to complementary information from the other; (2) a multi-resolution training strategy ( 10 m → 60 m → 10 m " role="presentation"> 10 m → 60 m → 10 m 10 m → 60 m → 10 m ) that fuses Sentinel-1/2 features at native resolution, aggregates to 60 m for supervision against Gap-Filled Fractional Snow Cover (GFSC) labels, and disaggregates through guided upsampling to restore fine spatial structure at 10 m " role="presentation"> 10 m 10 m ; and (3) quality-aware curriculum learning that incorporates multi-source metadata (cloud masks, atmospheric parameters, quality control flags) through adaptive loss weighting, enabling robust training from heterogeneous satellite data while providing calibrated uncertainty estimates. Evaluation on 29 Sentinel-1/2 tiles from Northern Finland (18,117 test patches from 4 geographically unseen tiles) demonstrates state-of-the-art performance: 13.77% MAE and 94.66% classification accuracy, representing 38.7% MAE reduction over the best baseline. Ablation studies confirm cross-attention as the largest individual contributor (5.16 percentage point accuracy gain), while uncertainty quantification achieves excellent calibration (ECE=0.025). The methodology generalizes to earth observation applications requiring fusion of heterogeneous multi-resolution satellite data

item.title: SFGFusion: Surface fitting guided 3D object detection with 4D radar and camera fusion
item.dc:creator: Xiaozhi Li
Huijun Di
Jian Li
Feng Liu
Wei Liang
item.description: 3D object detection is essential for autonomous driving. As an emerging sensor, 4D imaging radar offers advantages as low cost, long-range detection, and accurate velocity measurement, making it highly suitable for object detection. However, its sparse point clouds and low resolution limit object geometric representation and hinder multi-modal fusion. In this study, we introduce SFGFusion, a novel camera-4D imaging radar detection network guided by surface fitting. By estimating quadratic surface parameters of objects from image and radar data, the explicit surface fitting model enhances spatial representation and cross-modal interaction, enabling more reliable prediction of fine-grained dense depth. The predicted depth serves two purposes: 1) in an image branch to guide the transformation of image features from perspective view (PV) to a unified bird’s-eye view (BEV) for multi-modal fusion, improving spatial mapping accuracy; and 2) in a surface pseudo-point branch to generate dense pseudo-point cloud, mitigating the radar point sparsity. The original radar point cloud is also encoded in a separate radar branch. These two point cloud branches adopt a pillar-based method and subsequently transform the features into the BEV space. Finally, a standard 2D backbone and detection head are used to predict object labels and bounding boxes from BEV features. Experimental results show that SFGFusion effectively fuses camera and 4D radar features, achieving superior performance on the TJ4DRadSet and view-of-delft (VoD) object detection benchmarks. Published: 2026-05-22T06:53:02+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.789 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xiaozhi Li; Huijun Di; Jian Li; Feng Liu; Wei Liang

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113999

Relevance: 0.789 (must_read)

3D object detection is essential for autonomous driving. As an emerging sensor, 4D imaging radar offers advantages as low cost, long-range detection, and accurate velocity measurement, making it highly suitable for object detection. However, its sparse point clouds and low resolution limit object geometric representation and hinder multi-modal fusion. In this study, we introduce SFGFusion, a novel camera-4D imaging radar detection network guided by surface fitting. By estimating quadratic surface parameters of objects from image and radar data, the explicit surface fitting model enhances spatial representation and cross-modal interaction, enabling more reliable prediction of fine-grained dense depth. The predicted depth serves two purposes: 1) in an image branch to guide the transformation of image features from perspective view (PV) to a unified bird’s-eye view (BEV) for multi-modal fusion, improving spatial mapping accuracy; and 2) in a surface pseudo-point branch to generate dense pseudo-point cloud, mitigating the radar point sparsity. The original radar point cloud is also encoded in a separate radar branch. These two point cloud branches adopt a pillar-based method and subsequently transform the features into the BEV space. Finally, a standard 2D backbone and detection head are used to predict object labels and bounding boxes from BEV features. Experimental results show that SFGFusion effectively fuses camera and 4D radar features, achieving superior performance on the TJ4DRadSet and view-of-delft (VoD) object detection benchmarks.

item.title: Fuse only what matters: Degeneracy-aware multi-sensor fusion for LiDAR-Inertial-Visual SLAM
item.dc:creator: Xuanxuan Zhang
Jie Xu
Chengxi Yang
Guanyu Huang
Lijun Zhao
Ruifeng Li
Shenghai Yuan
You Li
Lihua Xie
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: Recent advances in LiDAR-Inertial-Visual SLAM typically adopt an “all-in” fusion strategy, indiscriminately integrating all sensor inputs regardless of necessity. While this approach aims to enhance robustness, it often incurs excessive computational overhead and can degrade performance by introducing noise from lower-precision modalities in non-degenerate scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose a degeneracy-aware framework anchored by a Selective Kalman Filter that fundamentally resolves “when” and “how” to fuse multi-modal data. Specifically, our approach introduces a rigorous detection metric that evaluates the coupled geometric constraints within the state space, enabling the filter to distinguish between actual system failure and benign environmental features. By accurately identifying the precise timing and direction of sensor degeneracy, our system selectively incorporates visual measurements on an on-demand basis. This mechanism integrates visual constraints exclusively into the dimensions where the primary LiDAR-inertial subsystem fails. Such a selective approach effectively eliminates redundant computation and prevents the contamination of high-precision states by less reliable visual data during nominal operation. Our extensive experimental validation spans a wide variety of challenging scenarios, including texture-less indoor corridors and large-scale open environments, verifying the system’s ability to maintain high localization accuracy where single-modality approaches typically fail. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that our method achieves superior localization accuracy and robustness while significantly enhancing computational efficiency compared to state-of-the-art “all-in” frameworks. Published: 2026-05-22T17:19:35+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.788 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xuanxuan Zhang; Jie Xu; Chengxi Yang; Guanyu Huang; Lijun Zhao; Ruifeng Li; Shenghai Yuan; You Li; Lihua Xie

Journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1016/j.isprsjprs.2026.05.031

Relevance: 0.788 (must_read)

Recent advances in LiDAR-Inertial-Visual SLAM typically adopt an “all-in” fusion strategy, indiscriminately integrating all sensor inputs regardless of necessity. While this approach aims to enhance robustness, it often incurs excessive computational overhead and can degrade performance by introducing noise from lower-precision modalities in non-degenerate scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose a degeneracy-aware framework anchored by a Selective Kalman Filter that fundamentally resolves “when” and “how” to fuse multi-modal data. Specifically, our approach introduces a rigorous detection metric that evaluates the coupled geometric constraints within the state space, enabling the filter to distinguish between actual system failure and benign environmental features. By accurately identifying the precise timing and direction of sensor degeneracy, our system selectively incorporates visual measurements on an on-demand basis. This mechanism integrates visual constraints exclusively into the dimensions where the primary LiDAR-inertial subsystem fails. Such a selective approach effectively eliminates redundant computation and prevents the contamination of high-precision states by less reliable visual data during nominal operation. Our extensive experimental validation spans a wide variety of challenging scenarios, including texture-less indoor corridors and large-scale open environments, verifying the system’s ability to maintain high localization accuracy where single-modality approaches typically fail. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that our method achieves superior localization accuracy and robustness while significantly enhancing computational efficiency compared to state-of-the-art “all-in” frameworks.

item.title: Sparse multimodal feature fusion transformer with frequency-convolution collaboration for wildlife multi-object tracking in UAV monitoring
item.dc:creator: Zixuan Yin
Yaqin Zhao
Panpan Li
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: Lightweight unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are gradually becoming important remote sensing tools for wildlife monitoring. Wildlife tracking using UAVs faces challenges, including small targets, highly similar appearances, and strong camouflage in natural environments. To address these issues, we propose a multimodal wildlife multi-object tracking model based on text–image feature fusion. First, the Density Multimodal Fusion (DTF) module combines spatial-density-guided sparse sampling with text priors to focus on high-density foregrounds while suppressing background redundancy. Second, the frequency-convolution collaborative encoder (LST-Encoder) performs spatially conditioned spectral modulation to enhance informative frequency components and preserve global semantics, improving discriminability for camouflaged and visually similar targets. Finally, the Hybrid-Decoder mitigates ID switches and identity fragmentation by integrating global spatiotemporal modeling with hierarchical identity modulation, maintaining cross-frame feature continuity and reinforcing identity-specific cues for stable long-sequence tracking. We validate our approach on the newly constructed MiluDrone dataset and public datasets. The experimental results show that the proposed method significantly outperforms several representative trackers, achieving a 4% increase in MOTA, a 3.7% increase in IDF1, and a 47% reduction in ID switches on the MiluDrone dataset. These results demonstrate the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed framework for challenging UAV-based wildlife monitoring scenarios. The code is available at https://github.com/yzxhao123/De-MOTR.git . Published: 2026-05-23T13:35:46+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.787 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zixuan Yin; Yaqin Zhao; Panpan Li

Journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1016/j.isprsjprs.2026.05.033

Relevance: 0.787 (must_read)

Lightweight unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are gradually becoming important remote sensing tools for wildlife monitoring. Wildlife tracking using UAVs faces challenges, including small targets, highly similar appearances, and strong camouflage in natural environments. To address these issues, we propose a multimodal wildlife multi-object tracking model based on text–image feature fusion. First, the Density Multimodal Fusion (DTF) module combines spatial-density-guided sparse sampling with text priors to focus on high-density foregrounds while suppressing background redundancy. Second, the frequency-convolution collaborative encoder (LST-Encoder) performs spatially conditioned spectral modulation to enhance informative frequency components and preserve global semantics, improving discriminability for camouflaged and visually similar targets. Finally, the Hybrid-Decoder mitigates ID switches and identity fragmentation by integrating global spatiotemporal modeling with hierarchical identity modulation, maintaining cross-frame feature continuity and reinforcing identity-specific cues for stable long-sequence tracking. We validate our approach on the newly constructed MiluDrone dataset and public datasets. The experimental results show that the proposed method significantly outperforms several representative trackers, achieving a 4% increase in MOTA, a 3.7% increase in IDF1, and a 47% reduction in ID switches on the MiluDrone dataset. These results demonstrate the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed framework for challenging UAV-based wildlife monitoring scenarios. The code is available at https://github.com/yzxhao123/De-MOTR.git .

item.title: Mixture of Disentangled Experts with Missing Modalities for Robust Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
item.dc:creator: Xiang Li
Xiaoming Zhang
Dezhuang Miao
Xianfu Cheng
Dawei Li
Honggui Han
Zhoujun Li
item.description: Multimodal Sentiment Analysis (MSA) integrates multiple modalities to infer human sentiment, but real-world noise often leads to missing or corrupted data. However, existing feature-disentangled methods struggle to handle the internal variations of heterogeneous information under uncertain missingness, making it difficult to learn effective multimodal representations from degraded modalities. To address this issue, we propose DERL , a D isentangled E xpert R epresentation L earning framework for robust MSA. Specifically, DERL employs hybrid experts to adaptively disentangle multimodal inputs into orthogonal private and shared representation spaces. A multi-level reconstruction strategy is further developed to provide collaborative supervision, enhancing both the expressiveness and robustness of the learned representations. Finally, the disentangled features act as modality experts with distinct roles to generate importance-aware fusion results. Extensive experiments on three MSA benchmarks demonstrate that DERL outperforms state-of-the-art methods under various missing-modality conditions. For instance, our method improves Acc-2 by 2.22% and reduces MAE by 2.25% on CMU-MOSI under intra-modal missingness. Published: 2026-05-22T05:55:29+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.787 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xiang Li; Xiaoming Zhang; Dezhuang Miao; Xianfu Cheng; Dawei Li; Honggui Han; Zhoujun Li

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104495

Relevance: 0.787 (must_read)

Multimodal Sentiment Analysis (MSA) integrates multiple modalities to infer human sentiment, but real-world noise often leads to missing or corrupted data. However, existing feature-disentangled methods struggle to handle the internal variations of heterogeneous information under uncertain missingness, making it difficult to learn effective multimodal representations from degraded modalities. To address this issue, we propose DERL , a D isentangled E xpert R epresentation L earning framework for robust MSA. Specifically, DERL employs hybrid experts to adaptively disentangle multimodal inputs into orthogonal private and shared representation spaces. A multi-level reconstruction strategy is further developed to provide collaborative supervision, enhancing both the expressiveness and robustness of the learned representations. Finally, the disentangled features act as modality experts with distinct roles to generate importance-aware fusion results. Extensive experiments on three MSA benchmarks demonstrate that DERL outperforms state-of-the-art methods under various missing-modality conditions. For instance, our method improves Acc-2 by 2.22% and reduces MAE by 2.25% on CMU-MOSI under intra-modal missingness.

item.title: Heterogeneous knowledge distillation fostered pre-training for remote sensing object detection
item.dc:creator: Fan Liu
Xinlei Zhang
Chuanyi Zhang
Liang Yao
Fan Cheng
Xinyuan Li
Xiaohan Yu
item.description: Most backbones of existing remote sensing object detectors are typically initialized with pre-trained weights on natural images from ImageNet. However, domain gaps exist between remote sensing and natural images, making backbones initialized with ImageNet pre-trained weights perform suboptimal generalization for remote sensing scenarios. To mitigate this issue, we propose a novel remote sensing backbone pre-training framework named HKDP (Heterogeneous Knowledge Distillation Fostered Pre-training) for object detection. Specifically, we employ heterogeneous distillation to effectively transfer domain-specific knowledge from an RS foundation model to lightweight backbones. Since objects in remote sensing images are typically orientation-sensitive, our HKDP incorporates a rotation distillation branch to learn rotation-invariant representations. Furthermore, to enrich the RS domain knowledge and facilitate this distillation process, we integrate 20 existing remote sensing classification datasets via an adaptive data sampling module and construct the most category-rich dataset—RS56K with 70 distinct categories. Extensive evaluations using ResNet-50 and PKINet-s backbones demonstrate that HKDP achieves superior performance on three benchmarks, including DIOR-R, SSDD+, and DOTA-v1.0. Code and dataset are available at https://github.com/BasilLeave/HKDP . Published: 2026-05-22T16:12:02+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.786 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Fan Liu; Xinlei Zhang; Chuanyi Zhang; Liang Yao; Fan Cheng; Xinyuan Li; Xiaohan Yu

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.114030

Relevance: 0.786 (must_read)

Most backbones of existing remote sensing object detectors are typically initialized with pre-trained weights on natural images from ImageNet. However, domain gaps exist between remote sensing and natural images, making backbones initialized with ImageNet pre-trained weights perform suboptimal generalization for remote sensing scenarios. To mitigate this issue, we propose a novel remote sensing backbone pre-training framework named HKDP (Heterogeneous Knowledge Distillation Fostered Pre-training) for object detection. Specifically, we employ heterogeneous distillation to effectively transfer domain-specific knowledge from an RS foundation model to lightweight backbones. Since objects in remote sensing images are typically orientation-sensitive, our HKDP incorporates a rotation distillation branch to learn rotation-invariant representations. Furthermore, to enrich the RS domain knowledge and facilitate this distillation process, we integrate 20 existing remote sensing classification datasets via an adaptive data sampling module and construct the most category-rich dataset—RS56K with 70 distinct categories. Extensive evaluations using ResNet-50 and PKINet-s backbones demonstrate that HKDP achieves superior performance on three benchmarks, including DIOR-R, SSDD+, and DOTA-v1.0. Code and dataset are available at https://github.com/BasilLeave/HKDP .

item.title: Enhancing knowledge distillation via difficult feature-based weighted loss
item.dc:creator: Pan He
Hang Ruan
Lei Zhang
item.description: As a key technology for model compression and performance improvement, the core framework of Knowledge Distillation (KD) involves leveraging a pre-trained teacher model to guide the training of a student model that is yet to be trained. However, existing feature-based KD methods adopt homogeneous learning strategies for different features during the training process of the student model. Specifically, they assign the same weight to each feature without making differentiated adjustments based on the inherent learning difficulty of the features themselves. This study proposes a Difficult Feature-based Weighted Loss (DF-WL) to enhance feature-based knowledge distillation. This mechanism quantifies the learning difficulty of different feature regions through pixel-level feature loss, and then generates weights based on the difficulty level—assigning higher weights to features with high learning difficulty and lower weights to easily learned features. This achieves a shift from homogeneous weights to hardness-aware dynamic weights. Ultimately, our method improves the distillation performance by strengthening the student network’s learning of difficult features. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-100 and ImageNet demonstrate that integrating the proposed DF-WL into existing mainstream knowledge distillation methods can further boost their performance and help them achieve more competitive results. Published: 2026-05-22T06:57:51+00:00 Venue: Knowledge-Based Systems Score: 0.784 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Pan He; Hang Ruan; Lei Zhang

Journal: Knowledge-Based Systems

DOI: 10.1016/j.knosys.2026.116247

Relevance: 0.784 (must_read)

As a key technology for model compression and performance improvement, the core framework of Knowledge Distillation (KD) involves leveraging a pre-trained teacher model to guide the training of a student model that is yet to be trained. However, existing feature-based KD methods adopt homogeneous learning strategies for different features during the training process of the student model. Specifically, they assign the same weight to each feature without making differentiated adjustments based on the inherent learning difficulty of the features themselves. This study proposes a Difficult Feature-based Weighted Loss (DF-WL) to enhance feature-based knowledge distillation. This mechanism quantifies the learning difficulty of different feature regions through pixel-level feature loss, and then generates weights based on the difficulty level—assigning higher weights to features with high learning difficulty and lower weights to easily learned features. This achieves a shift from homogeneous weights to hardness-aware dynamic weights. Ultimately, our method improves the distillation performance by strengthening the student network’s learning of difficult features. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-100 and ImageNet demonstrate that integrating the proposed DF-WL into existing mainstream knowledge distillation methods can further boost their performance and help them achieve more competitive results.

item.title: EntSeg: Entropy-Guided Pseudo-Label Denoising and Masked Image Consistency for Cross-Domain Remote Sensing Segmentation
item.dc:creator: Bin Wang
Zeyu Chen
Shun Lv
Fei Deng
Yiguang Liu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (UDA) for semantic segmentation of Remote Sensing Images (RSIs) aims to adapt a model trained on labeled source-domain data to a target domain without requiring target-domain annotations. Existing self-training (ST) approaches adapt the model by generating pseudo labels for target-domain samples. However, due to the inherent domain gap, these pseudo labels often contain substantial noise, which in turn limits the effectiveness of such methods. We observe that noisy pseudo labels typically exhibit higher entropy. Motivated by this observation, we propose entropy-guided segmentation (EntSeg) framework, a novel entropy-guided framework for cross-domain RSIs semantic segmentation, that comprising two key models. The entropy-weighted pseudo-label denoising (EPLD) module dynamically adjusts the learning weights of noisy pixel locations (i.e., pixels with high entropy) to mitigate the adverse influence of noisy pseudo labels on domain adaptation. Nevertheless, the dynamic weighting mechanism of EPLD also results in insufficient contextual learning for target-domain data, especially at locations assigned low weights. To address this issue, we introduce entropy-guided masked image consistency (EMIC), which employs an entropy-guided strategy to mask high-entropy regions and learns spatial contextual relationships in the target domain through masked image consistency, thereby enhancing the model’s robustness in visual recognition. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves highly competitive performance. The code will be released at https://github.com/woldier/EntSeg. Published: 2026-05-22T19:36:13+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.783 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Bin Wang; Zeyu Chen; Shun Lv; Fei Deng; Yiguang Liu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3696112

Relevance: 0.783 (must_read)

Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (UDA) for semantic segmentation of Remote Sensing Images (RSIs) aims to adapt a model trained on labeled source-domain data to a target domain without requiring target-domain annotations. Existing self-training (ST) approaches adapt the model by generating pseudo labels for target-domain samples. However, due to the inherent domain gap, these pseudo labels often contain substantial noise, which in turn limits the effectiveness of such methods. We observe that noisy pseudo labels typically exhibit higher entropy. Motivated by this observation, we propose entropy-guided segmentation (EntSeg) framework, a novel entropy-guided framework for cross-domain RSIs semantic segmentation, that comprising two key models. The entropy-weighted pseudo-label denoising (EPLD) module dynamically adjusts the learning weights of noisy pixel locations (i.e., pixels with high entropy) to mitigate the adverse influence of noisy pseudo labels on domain adaptation. Nevertheless, the dynamic weighting mechanism of EPLD also results in insufficient contextual learning for target-domain data, especially at locations assigned low weights. To address this issue, we introduce entropy-guided masked image consistency (EMIC), which employs an entropy-guided strategy to mask high-entropy regions and learns spatial contextual relationships in the target domain through masked image consistency, thereby enhancing the model’s robustness in visual recognition. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves highly competitive performance. The code will be released at https://github.com/woldier/EntSeg.

item.title: S2CMamba: Structure-to-Contrast Semantic Mamba for Hyperspectral and LiDAR Joint Classification
item.dc:creator: Niannian Zhang
Thomas Xinzhang Wu
Lianhui Liang
Bing Tu
Jun Li
Antonio Plaza
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Hyperspectral imaging (HSI) and light detection and ranging (LiDAR) provide complementary spectral and elevation information, making their fusion an effective solution for multimodal remote sensing (RS) classification. However, existing fusion methods often fail to adequately capture complex spatial topology and long-range contextual dependencies. Moreover, foreground–background semantic entanglement and the lack of semantic-guided fusion mechanisms further degrade feature discrimination. To address these challenges, a novel structure-to-contrast semantic Mamba (S2CMamba) network is proposed for the joint classification of HSI and LiDAR data. Specifically, a structure-aware state space modeling (S2SSM) module is designed for both HSI and LiDAR branches to encode multi-directional spatial structures and capture long-range global spatial dependencies within each modality. Then, the proposed semantic decoupling unit (SDU) explicitly separates foreground and background semantic responses in the fused HSI–LiDAR representations, which effectively suppresses background interference and alleviates semantic ambiguity during multimodal feature fusion. Based on the decoupled semantic priors, a contrast-guided feature aggregation module (CGAM) is developed to enable contrast-modulated local window aggregation over the fused HSI–LiDAR features, thereby improving intraclass compactness and enhancing class-boundary discrimination. Through experiments conducted on three commonly used HSI and LiDAR datasets, the performance of S2CMamba was consistently demonstrated to surpass that of state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods for HSI and LiDAR. Published: 2026-05-22T19:36:13+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.783 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Niannian Zhang; Thomas Xinzhang Wu; Lianhui Liang; Bing Tu; Jun Li; Antonio Plaza

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3696285

Relevance: 0.783 (must_read)

Hyperspectral imaging (HSI) and light detection and ranging (LiDAR) provide complementary spectral and elevation information, making their fusion an effective solution for multimodal remote sensing (RS) classification. However, existing fusion methods often fail to adequately capture complex spatial topology and long-range contextual dependencies. Moreover, foreground–background semantic entanglement and the lack of semantic-guided fusion mechanisms further degrade feature discrimination. To address these challenges, a novel structure-to-contrast semantic Mamba (S2CMamba) network is proposed for the joint classification of HSI and LiDAR data. Specifically, a structure-aware state space modeling (S2SSM) module is designed for both HSI and LiDAR branches to encode multi-directional spatial structures and capture long-range global spatial dependencies within each modality. Then, the proposed semantic decoupling unit (SDU) explicitly separates foreground and background semantic responses in the fused HSI–LiDAR representations, which effectively suppresses background interference and alleviates semantic ambiguity during multimodal feature fusion. Based on the decoupled semantic priors, a contrast-guided feature aggregation module (CGAM) is developed to enable contrast-modulated local window aggregation over the fused HSI–LiDAR features, thereby improving intraclass compactness and enhancing class-boundary discrimination. Through experiments conducted on three commonly used HSI and LiDAR datasets, the performance of S2CMamba was consistently demonstrated to surpass that of state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods for HSI and LiDAR.

item.title: RCGDet3D: Rethinking 4D Radar-Camera Fusion-based 3D Object Detection with Enhanced Radar Feature Encoding
item.dc:creator: Weiyi Xiong
Bing Zhu
item.description: 4D automotive radar is indispensable for autonomous driving due to its low cost and robustness, yet its point cloud sparsity challenges 3D object detection. Existing 4D radar-camera fusion methods focus on complex fusion strategies, trading inference speed for marginal gains. This trade-off hinders real-time deployment due to heavy computation on dense feature maps. In contrast, feature extraction from sparse radar points is less time-consuming but remains under-explored. This work uncovers that simply enhancing radar feature extraction can achieve comparable or even higher performance than elaborate fusion modules, while maintaining real-time performance. Based on this finding, we propose RCGDet3D, which centers on radar feature encoding and simplifies multi-modal fusion. Its encoder inherits from the efficient Gaussian Splatting-based Point Gaussian Encoder (PGE) in RadarGaussianDet3D with two key improvements. First, the Ray-centric PGE (R-PGE) predicts Gaussian attributes in ray-aligned coordinate systems before unifying them to Bird's-Eye View (BEV) space, significantly improving geometric consistency and reducing learning difficulty by decoupling the coordinate transformation from representation learning. Second, a Semantic Injection (SI) module incorporates visual cues from images, producing more geometrically accurate and semantically enriched radar features. Experiments on View-of-Delft (VoD) and TJ4DRadSet show that RCGDet3D outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both accuracy and speed, setting a new benchmark for real-time deployment. Published: 2026-05-20T12:45:01+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.783 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Weiyi Xiong; Bing Zhu

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.783 (must_read)

4D automotive radar is indispensable for autonomous driving due to its low cost and robustness, yet its point cloud sparsity challenges 3D object detection. Existing 4D radar-camera fusion methods focus on complex fusion strategies, trading inference speed for marginal gains. This trade-off hinders real-time deployment due to heavy computation on dense feature maps. In contrast, feature extraction from sparse radar points is less time-consuming but remains under-explored. This work uncovers that simply enhancing radar feature extraction can achieve comparable or even higher performance than elaborate fusion modules, while maintaining real-time performance. Based on this finding, we propose RCGDet3D, which centers on radar feature encoding and simplifies multi-modal fusion. Its encoder inherits from the efficient Gaussian Splatting-based Point Gaussian Encoder (PGE) in RadarGaussianDet3D with two key improvements. First, the Ray-centric PGE (R-PGE) predicts Gaussian attributes in ray-aligned coordinate systems before unifying them to Bird's-Eye View (BEV) space, significantly improving geometric consistency and reducing learning difficulty by decoupling the coordinate transformation from representation learning. Second, a Semantic Injection (SI) module incorporates visual cues from images, producing more geometrically accurate and semantically enriched radar features. Experiments on View-of-Delft (VoD) and TJ4DRadSet show that RCGDet3D outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both accuracy and speed, setting a new benchmark for real-time deployment.

item.title: Structured grouping collaborative decorrelated regularization for model pruning in infrared small target detection
item.dc:creator: Yonghao Li
Jun Chen
Boyang Li
Yulan Guo
Longguang Wang
Siyi Deng
item.description: To address the critical challenge of deep learning networks in resource-limited scenarios, network pruning has emerged as a powerful technique for removing redundant network structures and enhancing computational efficiency. In this pioneering work on Infrared Small Target Detection (IRSTD), we introduce network pruning to enhance the efficiency of IRSTD networks. However, the direct application of existing pruning methods on IRSTD is hampered by the challenges of low signal-to-noise ratios or lack of semantic information, leading to suboptimal performance. To achieve effective pruning without sacrificing accuracy, this paper proposes a Structure Grouping Collaborative Decorrelated Regularization Pruning (SGDP) method. Specifically, we first propose the Shield Layers (SL) to avoid pruning important modules. Then, a Hierarchical Coupling Structure Grouping strategy (HCSG) is proposed to explore the hierarchical dependencies among different layers. Strongly correlated channels are either considered to be maintained or pruned. After that, a Decorrelated L2-norm Regularization (DL2R) pruning strategy is designed to apply progressive L2-norm regularization constraints to redundant substructures while jointly optimizing layer-wise biases and weights. Experimental results on the benchmark datasets (e.g., NUDT-SIRST and NUAA-SIRST) demonstrate that our method even achieves comparable detection performance with the original model under high pruning rate. Moreover, deployment experiments on edge devices (NVIDIA AGX and HUAWEI Ascend-310B) show that our method can achieve over 2× inference acceleration compared with the original model. Our code will be released on https://github.com/lyh-nudt/SGDP . Published: 2026-05-22T16:11:30+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.782 (must_read)

Authors: Yonghao Li; Jun Chen; Boyang Li; Yulan Guo; Longguang Wang; Siyi Deng

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.114005

Relevance: 0.782 (must_read)

To address the critical challenge of deep learning networks in resource-limited scenarios, network pruning has emerged as a powerful technique for removing redundant network structures and enhancing computational efficiency. In this pioneering work on Infrared Small Target Detection (IRSTD), we introduce network pruning to enhance the efficiency of IRSTD networks. However, the direct application of existing pruning methods on IRSTD is hampered by the challenges of low signal-to-noise ratios or lack of semantic information, leading to suboptimal performance. To achieve effective pruning without sacrificing accuracy, this paper proposes a Structure Grouping Collaborative Decorrelated Regularization Pruning (SGDP) method. Specifically, we first propose the Shield Layers (SL) to avoid pruning important modules. Then, a Hierarchical Coupling Structure Grouping strategy (HCSG) is proposed to explore the hierarchical dependencies among different layers. Strongly correlated channels are either considered to be maintained or pruned. After that, a Decorrelated L2-norm Regularization (DL2R) pruning strategy is designed to apply progressive L2-norm regularization constraints to redundant substructures while jointly optimizing layer-wise biases and weights. Experimental results on the benchmark datasets (e.g., NUDT-SIRST and NUAA-SIRST) demonstrate that our method even achieves comparable detection performance with the original model under high pruning rate. Moreover, deployment experiments on edge devices (NVIDIA AGX and HUAWEI Ascend-310B) show that our method can achieve over 2× inference acceleration compared with the original model. Our code will be released on https://github.com/lyh-nudt/SGDP .

item.title: Unified skeleton representation learning for autonomous driving
item.dc:creator: Mohamed Abdelfattah
Alexandre Alahi
item.prism:publicationName: Transportation Research Part C: Emerging Technologies
item.description: How can a model learn human skeleton representations that generalize across multiple tasks in autonomous driving and intelligent transportation systems? Traditional self-supervised methods fall short by entangling spatial and temporal features, limiting their adaptability to downstream perception and behavior understanding tasks. In this paper, we introduce MotionMoCo , a novel self-supervised framework designed to decouple spatial and temporal information for robust, transferable skeleton representations. MotionMoCo consists of two branches, a query branch and a momentum-updated key branch, each of which separately encodes the spatial, temporal, and combined features from the input motion sequence. Unlike previous works, we contrast these features independently across the two branches, thus preserving their complementary information content. Further, we propose a Motion-Aware Frame Masking strategy that selectively removes fast-evolving frames in the query branch to encourage robust feature extraction. MotionMoCo serves as a generic pretraining stage that can be fine-tuned for a range of autonomous driving applications, including pedestrian intention prediction, skeleton-based action recognition, 2D-to-3D pose lifting, and human mesh recovery, demonstrating that spatiotemporal disentanglement enhances the quality and adaptability of skeleton representations. Published: 2026-05-23T00:02:22+00:00 Venue: Transportation Research Part C: Emerging Technologies Score: 0.782 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Mohamed Abdelfattah; Alexandre Alahi

Journal: Transportation Research Part C: Emerging Technologies

DOI: 10.1016/j.trc.2026.105753

Relevance: 0.782 (must_read)

How can a model learn human skeleton representations that generalize across multiple tasks in autonomous driving and intelligent transportation systems? Traditional self-supervised methods fall short by entangling spatial and temporal features, limiting their adaptability to downstream perception and behavior understanding tasks. In this paper, we introduce MotionMoCo , a novel self-supervised framework designed to decouple spatial and temporal information for robust, transferable skeleton representations. MotionMoCo consists of two branches, a query branch and a momentum-updated key branch, each of which separately encodes the spatial, temporal, and combined features from the input motion sequence. Unlike previous works, we contrast these features independently across the two branches, thus preserving their complementary information content. Further, we propose a Motion-Aware Frame Masking strategy that selectively removes fast-evolving frames in the query branch to encourage robust feature extraction. MotionMoCo serves as a generic pretraining stage that can be fine-tuned for a range of autonomous driving applications, including pedestrian intention prediction, skeleton-based action recognition, 2D-to-3D pose lifting, and human mesh recovery, demonstrating that spatiotemporal disentanglement enhances the quality and adaptability of skeleton representations.

item.title: Hybrid linear attention: A vision transformer integrating selective sampling softmax and multi-feature fusion enhancement
item.dc:creator: Senqi Guan
Wenxin Liang
Yunlong Gao
Linlin Zong
Xinyue Liu
Xianchao Zhang
item.description: Transformer models have achieved remarkable success across various domains, with their superior performance primarily stemming from the innovative attention mechanism. However, the quadratic complexity of the attention mechanism has hindered further development. Window-based attention addresses this by dividing feature maps into local windows, but restricts long-range information interaction. Linear attention alters Q K V " role="presentation"> Q K V Q K V computation order to achieve linear complexity while preserving long-range interactions-but it loses the ability to emphasize critical regions and diminishes feature diversity. To address these issues, we propose a hybrid linear attention mechanism. First, we design a Selective Sampling Softmax attention to efficiently emphasize essential regions with constrained computational costs. Second, we propose a feature enhancement module, which integrates channel attention, focused linear attention, and token interaction to enrich feature diversity. Third, we develop an adaptive feature fusion module to integrate complementary attention features, further enhancing model performance. Finally, based on the proposed Hybrid Linear Attention, we construct a series of Vision Transformer models named HLSM-ViTs. Classification experiments on ImageNet-1K, along with object detection and instance segmentation experiments on COCO 2017, show that HLSM-ViTs effectively balance performance and efficiency, highlighting their strong potential for downstream tasks. Our code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/HLSM-ViT/ . Published: 2026-05-22T23:29:49+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.782 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Senqi Guan; Wenxin Liang; Yunlong Gao; Linlin Zong; Xinyue Liu; Xianchao Zhang

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.114037

Relevance: 0.782 (must_read)

Transformer models have achieved remarkable success across various domains, with their superior performance primarily stemming from the innovative attention mechanism. However, the quadratic complexity of the attention mechanism has hindered further development. Window-based attention addresses this by dividing feature maps into local windows, but restricts long-range information interaction. Linear attention alters Q K V " role="presentation"> Q K V Q K V computation order to achieve linear complexity while preserving long-range interactions-but it loses the ability to emphasize critical regions and diminishes feature diversity. To address these issues, we propose a hybrid linear attention mechanism. First, we design a Selective Sampling Softmax attention to efficiently emphasize essential regions with constrained computational costs. Second, we propose a feature enhancement module, which integrates channel attention, focused linear attention, and token interaction to enrich feature diversity. Third, we develop an adaptive feature fusion module to integrate complementary attention features, further enhancing model performance. Finally, based on the proposed Hybrid Linear Attention, we construct a series of Vision Transformer models named HLSM-ViTs. Classification experiments on ImageNet-1K, along with object detection and instance segmentation experiments on COCO 2017, show that HLSM-ViTs effectively balance performance and efficiency, highlighting their strong potential for downstream tasks. Our code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/HLSM-ViT/ .

item.title: Deviation-guided dual-model machine learning SAR coherence change detection framework for extracting changes in low-coherence areas
item.dc:creator: Dayuan Liu
Timo Balz
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: Coherence Change Detection (CCD) is a crucial technique for monitoring surface dynamics in Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR), and it has wide-ranging applications in natural disaster monitoring and urban analysis. However, conventional CCD methods based on dual-temporal coherence typically rely on a single coherence estimate for decision-making. This makes it difficult to effectively distinguish between decoherence caused by real surface changes and that induced by other factors, such as geometric decorrelation, temporal decorrelation, system noise, and co-registration inaccuracies. To address this issue, this study proposes a novel dual-model framework for coherence change detection that integrates machine learning with coherence and deviation modeling. The proposed framework first employs a machine learning (ML) model for coherence estimation, followed by a deviation prediction model that learns the mapping between statistical features and estimation errors. The SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) method is then applied to interpret the deviation model. Based on the joint output of coherence and deviation estimates, a dual-threshold segmentation strategy is adopted to effectively extract low-coherence regions caused by genuine surface changes. Experimental results confirm the method’s robustness in small-sample and low-coherence scenarios. Validation with real-world data across different resolutions and scenarios confirms the method’s ability to accurately identify low-coherence regions caused by changes. By jointly utilizing coherence and deviation information, the proposed framework enhances detection accuracy and interpretability, providing a robust and reliable new CCD solution. Published: 2026-05-23T16:47:43+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.782 (must_read)
